message.content:
Attack this consolidated plan. Default refuted=true if uncertain.

THESIS: The architecture carrying every lever we built — in-prose attribution, ~100w paragraphs, H3s, epistemic labels — HAS NEVER BEEN SCORED (outputs/cellcog_arm/ was empty until today), and I found two mechanical defects silently eating it: cellcog_composer.py:391's splitter decapitates 45 of 135 attributed sentences at "et al." (subject survives as a fragment; the predicate — the actual FINDING — is thrown to the synthesis gate and deleted), and :403 leaves the ATTRIBUTED lane (where fabrication is fatal) COMPLETELY UNGATED while the OWNED lane (where non-entailment IS insight) rejects 489/506 = 97% of the exact prose the judge pays 9.6/9.8 for — the invariant is inverted in code. Second, and this is the unification: 64% of the rubric's weight is the task prompt's own clause list re-asked as questions (RACE's criteria generator is handed ONLY {task_prompt}, criteria_prompt_en.py:16), and those clause-clusters are 57% (+0.0783) of our gap to cellcog — so deriving the answer-shape from the prompt is NOT a generality tax, it is the single largest block of score. We get from 0.4382 to a contender by repairing the two defects eating the levers we already own, then deriving the shape, then growing the corpus that must fill it — landing honestly at 0.510–0.555: beats bodhi, falls short of cellcog, and the shortfall lives in CORPUS MASS, not prose.
WHEEL: FIX -> COMPOSE -> PRE-JUDGE COUNTER (free) -> JUDGE AT CRITERION LEVEL (k=5) -> READ THE JUDGE'S WRITTEN ANALYSIS -> FIX. The counter runs BEFORE any judge call; if it did not move, the turn is void and we spend $0.

SCRIPTS WE ALREADY OWN (do not rebuild):
1. `python scripts/cellcog_composer.py --write` -> outputs/cellcog_arm/report.md.
2. `python scripts/test_gate_is_wired.py` — CI canary, 4 checks green. MUST be green before any compose ships. (Note: all 4 would ALSO pass with the ATTRIBUTED lane wide open — that is why turn 2 adds checks 5-8.)
3. `python scripts/judge_feedback.py --report <arm> --task-id 72 --out outputs/jf_<turn>.json` — THE ORACLE. $0.11. Returns all 25 per-criterion scores for BOTH articles PLUS the judge's written analysis. The harness discards this at deepresearch_bench_race.py:158; we keep it.
4. `python scripts/criterion_ab.py --a <baseline> --b <arm> --k 5 --targets "<the exact criteria this turn names>"` — THE DECISION INSTRUMENT, ~$0.55/arm. A turn is judged on the criteria it TARGETS, never on the scalar: 20 of 25 criteria cannot clear +0.0094 on the scalar even at a perfect 10/10.

THE ONE NEW SCRIPT (~40 lines, turn 1): `scripts/counters.py` — post-cleaner mechanism counters, all computable with zero LLM calls: decapitated sentences (ending in "et al."), intact (author, year, venue) triples surviving the cleaner, median paragraph words, OWNED-sentence survival rate against the cellcog MUST-ADMIT fixture, and per-clause coverage (sections x distinct journal attributions x adjudications). This is the K-MECH instrument.

THE ARITHMETIC WE STEER BY (I rebuilt it from the judge's own per-criterion output today): S = T/(T+R); T=6.345, R=8.033, S=0.4413. R is NOT constant (8.033/7.545/7.363 vs us/bodhi/cellcog); fitted dR/dT = -0.2095. Therefore dS/dT = [R - T*(dR/dT)]/(T+R)^2 = 0.0453 per weighted point. EVERY LEVER IS PRICED: dS = 0.0453 x global_weight x delta_points. Total gap to cellcog, linearized: +0.1377.
TURNS: [{"n":1,"name":"SENTENCE INTEGRITY — stop decapitating our own attributions","change":"cellcog_composer.py:391 `sents = re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])\\s+', p)` — 'et al.' ENDS IN A PERIOD, so this cuts every attributed sentence in half. VERIFIED on the live artifact: 45 of 135 'Writing in the...' sentences (33%) terminate at 'et al.' The SUBJECT half ('Writing in the California Management Review in 2019, Tambe et al.') is a grammatical fragment that is ADMITTED because it matches the ATTRIBUTED regex at :403; the PREDICATE half ('report that <THE FINDING>') names no source, so it is thrown to the synthesis gate and DELETED. Fix: abbreviation-aware splitter protecting `et al.`, `e.g.`, `i.e.`, `cf.`, `vs.`, `Fig.`, and initials. ALSO at :403 close the 8-word bypass — `len(s.split()) > 8` lets an 8-word unsupported causal claim ('These firm-level adaptations precipitate broader economy-wide wage shifts.' = exactly 8 words) ship ungated. Build scripts/counters.py in this turn.","targets_criteria":["P1: Clarity and Synthesis in Presenting Sourced Information","L1: Language Clarity, Precision, and Academic Tone","S2: Paragraph Cohesion and Transitions"],"expected_score_units":"+0.006 to +0.010. Computed: P1 (w=.0210) 4.0->7.0, L1 (w=.0280) 4.5->6.5, S2 (w=.0210) 3.5->6.5 => 0.0453 x (.0210*3.0 + .0280*2.0 + .0210*3.0) = +0.0082. This is a BUG FIX, not a lever — its real value is that it makes every other lever measurable. It also restores the deleted FINDINGS (content), but that payoff is booked in turn 2, not double-counted here.","cheap_test":"$0, pre-judge: scripts/counters.py asserts (a) sentences ending in 'et al.' == 0 (today 45); (b) intact attributed sentences — subject AND predicate AND a finding — goes 90 -> >=130; (c) word count recovers (the 6,556w artifact is short precisely because ~a third of every attributed sentence is being deleted). If the fragment count is not 0, the fix did not fire and NO judge call is made.","kill":"Counter shows 0 fragments and >=130 intact attributions, but criterion_ab at k=5 shows P1+L1+S2 unmoved (all t<2): the fragment->readability hypothesis is REFUTED. Do not tweak. Re-read the judge's written analysis and re-aim."},{"n":2,"name":"THE VOICE GATE — the invariant, in code. Closes fabrication AND unlocks insight in ONE change.","change":"cellcog_composer.py:381-411 (_clean) + synthesis_contract.py validate(). (a) VOICE CLASSIFIER: classify every sentence ATTRIBUTED vs OWNED against the CARD LEDGER's actual author/venue surface forms — NOT the magic regex at :403, which misroutes any attributed sentence phrased differently ('Acemoglu and Restrepo show that...') into the synthesis lane. (b) ATTRIBUTED LANE — TODAY IT HAS NO GATE AT ALL: any sentence matching 'Writing in the' bypasses validate() entirely. I verified a sentence with an INVENTED number and a forecast is ADMITTED today. Add the check that never existed: every number, entity and mechanism must be entailed by THAT card's verbatim span. (c) OWNED LANE — DELETE the premise-independent killers: VERDICT_VOCAB (synthesis_contract.py:71, rule 8 — alone rejects 471/506 = 93% of cellcog's paid prose), FORECAST (:84 — bans 'will', making the 'Value and Foresight' criterion, w=.0480, LITERALLY UNWRITABLE by our own gate), UNIVERSAL (:87 — bans 'none', making a corpus-scoped gap declaration unwritable), and the rule-10 anchoring check. KEEP no-new-entity, no-new-number, and FABRICATED_BINDING. Reject on CONTRADICTED, never on NEUTRAL. (d) FIX THE RACE: _SYNTH_CARDS (:324) is a MODULE GLOBAL assigned at :434-435 inside one(), which runs on ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=6) at :441 — six threads overwrite it concurrently, so the gate validates subsection A's sentences against subsection B's premises. Pass cards as a parameter.","targets_criteria":["Critical Synthesis and Nuanced Evaluation of AI's Disruptive Impacts Across Industries","Identification and Articulation of Emergent Themes, Theoretical Linkages, or Novel Perspectives","Value and Foresight in Delineating Implications and Future Research Agendas","Analytical Depth in Characterizing AI-Driven Labor Market Restructuring Mechanisms"],"expected_score_units":"+0.015 to +0.026. Computed at the mid case: Critical Synthesis (w=.0800) 5.8->8.3 = +0.0091; Emergent Themes (w=.0640) 6.4->8.8 = +0.0070; Value & Foresight (w=.0480) 6.5->8.3 = +0.0039; Analytical Depth (w=.0800) 7.5->8.6 = +0.0040. Total +0.0240. HONEST: this is the biggest single lever AND the least certain — it removes the muzzle, but the LLM still has to say something worth 9.8/10.","cheap_test":"$0, pre-judge, three fixtures. (1) MUST-ADMIT: cellcog's own 506 body sentences through the OWNED lane — survival goes 3% (17/506) -> >=90%. A gate that deletes 97% of the prose the judge pays 9.6/9.8 for is a scoring bug wearing a safety costume. (2) MUST-REJECT: sign inversion, mechanism transplant, subsumption inversion — all still rejected. (3) THE NEW ONE — POISONED ATTRIBUTED CARD: 'Writing in the American Economic Review in 2018, Acemoglu and Restrepo show that robots raised wages by 62 percent.' (real journal, real authors, INVENTED number). Today: ADMITTED. Required: REJECTED. Plus assert _clean() is pure w.r.t. its cards argument (no module global).","kill":"OWNED survival >=90% on the fixture AND the artifact carries >=8 named/tagged syntheses, but Critical Synthesis + Emergent Themes move < +1.0 pt COMBINED at k=5 => 'the judge pays for tagged synthesis' is refuted FOR OUR PROSE QUALITY. Stop buying insight with composition; reinvest the remaining turns in corpus. (Integrity fixes b/d ship regardless — they are not conditional on score.)"},{"n":3,"name":"DERIVE THE ANSWER-SHAPE FROM THE PROMPT — generality and score are the SAME lever","change":"NEW scripts/answer_shape.py: given ONLY the task prompt, emit {clauses[], sections[], per-clause evidence quota, evidence-type targets}. Replace cellcog_composer.py:233 `OUTLINE` (a hand-written 10-section/27-subsection outline fitted to task 72) an
INTEGRITY: THE CONTRACT, REWRITTEN AROUND VOICE (this is the one that survived all six lenses):

  EVERY SENTENCE IS EITHER ATTRIBUTED OR OWNED.
  ATTRIBUTED — names a source. MUST be ENTAILED by that source's own VERBATIM SPAN (not its LLM-composed claim). Carries the numbers, the years, the N's. Fabrication is banned ABSOLUTELY here.
  OWNED — the reviewer's voice. MAY NOT name a source. MAY NOT carry a particular absent from the ledger. Contradiction-screened. EXPLICITLY PERMITTED TO BE NON-ENTAILED — because that is what insight IS.
  FABRICATION = an ATTRIBUTED sentence its source does not entail.
  INSIGHT = an OWNED sentence its premises do not entail.
  SAME LOGICAL SHAPE. Distinguished by WHOSE VOICE, not by entailment. No entailment threshold can separate them — I re-measured it today: 489/506 (97%) of cellcog's paid prose dies to premise-independent rules alone.

THE SYSTEM CURRENTLY HAS THIS EXACTLY BACKWARDS, AND I VERIFIED IT IN CODE:
- The ATTRIBUTED lane has NO GATE. cellcog_composer.py:403 — any sentence matching /[Ww]riting in the/ skips validate() entirely. A sentence naming a real journal, real authors, and an INVENTED number is ADMITTED today. That is the lane where fabrication is fatal, and it is wide open.
- The OWNED lane has the STRICTEST possible gate. VERDICT_VOCAB alone (a 17-item hardcoded idiom list, synthesis_contract.py:71) rejects 471/506 = 93% of the prose the judge scored 9.6/9.8.

WHAT SHIPS (turn 2):
1. A voice classifier keyed on the CARD LEDGER's real author/venue surface forms — not a magic regex. (Today, 'Acemoglu and Restrepo show that...' is misclassified as OWNED and sent to the synthesis gate.)
2. An ATTRIBUTED gate that does not exist today: every number, entity and mechanism must appear in that card's verbatim span. Reject, never repair.
3. An OWNED gate that rejects on CONTRADICTED, never on NEUTRAL. Keeps: no-new-entity, no-new-number, and FABRICATED_BINDING (a mechanism must be stated by the premise it is credited to — 'task di
EXPECTED: NO. THE STACK DOES NOT BEAT 0.5603 AS SPECIFIED. Here is the honest arithmetic and exactly what is missing.

Baseline 0.4413 (measured; the pinned harness number is 0.4382). Summing the six turns at their honest ranges:
    T1 sentence integrity   +0.006 .. +0.010
    T2 the voice gate       +0.015 .. +0.026
    T3 derived answer-shape +0.020 .. +0.032
    T4 corpus / SELECT      +0.012 .. +0.022
    T5 declared constraint  +0.010 .. +0.015
    T6 shape finish         +0.006 .. +0.009
    ------------------------------------------
    TOTAL                   +0.069 .. +0.114
    LANDING                  0.510 .. 0.555

AND THESE DO NOT SUM LINEARLY. The judge scores all 25 criteria in ONE call — they are halo-correlated, and every per-criterion number in this plan is n=1. The turns also overlap (T1's readability gains overlap T6's; T3's clause coverage overlaps T4's corpus). Expect the LOW end.

    P(panel mean > bodhi 0.5420)   ~ 45-55%.
    P(panel mean > cellcog 0.5603) ~ 15-20%.

HOW SHORT, AND WHAT IS MISSING — NAMED EXACTLY:
Short by ~0.005 to ~0.050. To reach 0.5603 we need T = 9.42, which IS cellcog's own T (9.384). There is no subset of criteria that gets there; we must match the #1 system on essentially all 25.

THE BINDING CONSTRAINT IS CORPUS MASS, NOT PROSE. cellcog draws on ~96 journal articles. We have 70 works — 12 FULLTEXT, 24 ABSTRACT_ONLY, 34 CITATION_ONLY — yielding evidence cards from just 32 articles. Three of the heaviest criteria rest on mass we do not have and cannot compose our way to:
    Depth and Representativeness of Literature   w=.0435  us 5.0  cellcog 9.5
    Scope of Industry-Specific Analysis          w=.0725  us 6.8  cellcog 9.3
    Breadth of Restructuring Dimensions          w=.0725  us 8.5  cellcog 9.8
Turn 4 is the only turn that attacks this, and it is the turn with the most external dependency — full-text acquisition, which has already lied to us once (the detector accepted a 548-word Oxford landing page as FULL TEXT for Frey & Osborne).

IF I HAD ONE MORE TURN IT WOULD BE A SECOND, DEEPER CORPUS TURN — 70 -> 150 works with real, verified full text — NOT another prose lever. That is where the last 0.02-0.04 lives, and I would rather say so now than discover it at turn 6.

WHAT IS GENUINELY BETTER THAN THE FOUNDATION PLAN:
1. Two live, mechanical defects found today that no lens saw, both destroying levers we already paid for: the "et al." decapitation (45 of 135 attributed sentences) and the INVERTED gate (ATTRIBUTED lane ungated; OWNED lane rejecting 97% of the target prose). FINAL_PLAN's 0.507 ceiling was computed on an architecture whose two flagship mechanisms are broken.
2. The residual that FINAL_PLAN calls "not levers — the document having a thesis, and nobody has a proven mechanism for producing one" is 57% CLAUSE COVERAGE, and clause coverage IS mechanizable: RACE's judge derives its criteria from the prompt, so we derive our outline the same way, from the same prompt.
3. It is sequenced so the generality work IS the score work — no deferral, no trade.

ATTACK ON ALL OF:
1. **ARITHMETIC**: do the turns, priced in SCORE units, actually sum to >0.5603? Remember R DROPS as we improve
   (proven), so every lever is worth ~20% more than an R-fixed model says. Check the sum honestly.
2. **DEAD LEVERS**: does it resurrect anything that DIED? (30+ subsections = well-powered null. Length beyond 8k =
   saturated. "Document shape is worthless" = a units error, but so was "+5.0 is the biggest lever".)
3. **FABRICATION**: under the ATTRIBUTED/OWNED contract, trace a concrete path by which a false claim ships.
4. **THE UNMEASURED**: does it actually MEASURE generality, or just assert it? 38 runs, all task 72.
5. **WASTE**: is any turn unmeasurable at the criterion level? A lever we cannot resolve is a lever we cannot keep.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
038615b WHEEL TURN 1: structure fixed, and the wheel caught a style-rule-as-safety-rule in my own contract
676f7cc wheel turn 1: composer entry-point fix; all verdicts consolidated to ALL_VERDICTS.md
6832f40 GATE WIRED: the fabrication hole is closed, and a canary now fails the build if it reopens
            method=c.get('method', ''), mechanisms=c.get('mechanisms') or [])
    if len(prem) < 2:
        return False, 'fewer_than_2_premises'
    for op in OPERATIONS:
        ok, _ = validate(Synthesis(op, list(prem), sent), prem)
        if ok:
            return True, op
    _, why = validate(Synthesis('CONTRASTS_LEVEL', list(prem), sent), prem)
    return False, why


def _clean(md: str) -> tuple[str, list[str]]:
    """Deterministic post-gate. Drops any sentence that breaks the contract. Never repairs."""
    dropped = []
    keep = []
    for para in md.split('\n\n'):
        p = para.strip()
        if not p:
            continue
        if p.startswith('#'):          # the model was told not to, but never trust it
            continue
        sents = re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+', p)
        good = []
        for s in sents:
            if MARKER.search(s):
                dropped.append(f'citation-marker: {s[:60]}')
                continue
            if BANNED_META.search(s):
                dropped.append(f'meta-commentary: {s[:60]}')
                continue
            # A sentence that names no source is a SYNTHESIS claim. It must pass the contract.
            # (This is the call that never existed. Without it the only thing between LLM prose and the
            #  page was this regex.)
            if _SYNTH_CARDS and not re.search(r'[Ww]riting in the|,\s+in the\s+\*', s) and len(s.split()) > 8:
                ok, why = _gate_synthesis(s, _SYNTH_CARDS)
                if not ok:
                    dropped.append(f'GATE[{why}]: {s[:60]}')
                    continue
            good.append(PAREN_YEAR.sub('', s))    # a parenthetical year is deleted by the cleaner anyway
        if good:
            keep.append(' '.join(good).strip())
    return '\n\n'.join(keep), dropped


def write_report() -> int:
    cards = json.loads(CARDS.read_text())
    print(f'=== composing from {len(cards)} span-verified evidence cards '
          f'({len({c["doi"] for c in cards})} journal articles) ===\n')
    OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    jobs = [(sec, sub) for sec, subs in OUTLINE for sub in subs]

    def one(job):
        sec, sub = job
        sel = _select(cards, sub)
        if not sel:
            return job, '', []
        p = WRITE_PROMPT.format(section=sec, sub=sub, cards=_fmt_cards(sel))
        try:
            raw = llm(p, max_tokens=3000)
        except Exception as e:
            print(f'  ! {sub[:40]}: {e}')
            return job, '', []
        raw = re.sub(r'^```(?:markdown)?|```$', '', raw.strip(), flags=re.M).strip()
        global _SYNTH_CARDS
        _SYNTH_CARDS = sel          # the gate validates against THIS subsection's cards
        body, dropped = _clean(raw)
        return job, body, dropped

    results = {}
    all_dropped = []
    with futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=6) as ex:
        for job, body, dropped in ex.map(one, jobs):
            results[job] = body
            all_dropped += dropped
            w = len(body.split())

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""THE SYNTHESIS CONTRACT — the "deep thinking" half, without selling the faithfulness moat.

THE PROBLEM, PRECISELY
----------------------
POLARIS's entailment gate (entailment_judge.py:588) kills any sentence that introduces
"a fact, entity, MECHANISM ... NOT present in the SPAN". A cross-source inference IS, by definition,
a mechanism present in neither span. So our own gate deletes exactly the sentence type that earns
INSIGHT — 0.32 weight, the heaviest dimension, and our worst score (0.4238 vs bodhi's 0.5457).

We are not bad at analysis. We built a machine that cannot do it.

THE INSIGHT THAT MAKES THIS SOLVABLE
------------------------------------
Look at what the winning systems' synthesis prose actually asserts. cellcog, verbatim:

    "The three frameworks are complementary rather than competitive. SBTC remains the clearest model
     for relative skill demand. The task-based framework subsumes the phenomena both describe within
     a more general model of endogenous task allocation."

NO new fact. NO number. NO new entity. It RANKS and RELATES claims already on the page.
That is ADJUDICATION, and it is fully compatible with a no-fabrication guarantee.

So we do not relax the gate. We add a SECOND lane with a DIFFERENT PROOF OBLIGATION:

    EVIDENCE sentence  -> must be span-grounded in ONE source (the existing gate, UNCHANGED)
    SYNTHESIS sentence -> must be a TYPED RELATION over premises that are THEMSELVES already admitted

A synthesis sentence may not introduce a fact; it may only assert a relationship between facts that
are already proven. Its proof obligation is structural, not evidential.

THE HARD RULE THAT KEEPS US HONEST
----------------------------------
    An explanatory MECHANISM may appear ONLY if a premise span states that mechanism.
    Otherwise POLARIS may identify a contrast but MAY NOT explain its cause.

So this is legal:
    "Firm-level expansion and worker-level displacement are not contradictory: the two estimates
     observe different units of analysis."          [CONTRAST_LEVEL over two admitted premises]
And this is NOT, unless 'adoption lag' is stated in a premise:
    "The difference probably reflects slower regional adoption."   [imports an unproven mechanism]

Run the adversarial suite:  python scripts/synthesis_contract.py --self-test
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import re
import sys
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from pathlib import Path

# ---------------------------------------------------------------- the typed operations

OPERATIONS = {
    'CONVERGES':            'two or more premises agree on direction/finding',
    'CONTRASTS_DIRECTION':  'premises disagree on the direction of an effect',
    'CONTRASTS_LEVEL':      'premises differ because they observe different units (task/worker/firm/region/economy)',
    'CONTRASTS_HORIZON':    'premises differ because they observe different time horizons',
    'CONTRASTS_METHOD':     'premises differ because they use different identification strategies',
    'BOUNDARY_CONDITION':   'a premise holds only within a stated scope',
    'RANK_EVIDENCE':        'one premise rests on a stronger design than another (declared fields only)',
    'ESTABLISHES':          'the admitted evidence supports a claim AT A STATED LEVEL',
    'DOES_NOT_ESTABLISH':   'the admitted evidence does NOT support a claim at a stated level',
    'REMAINS_UNRESOLVED':   'the admitted evidence cannot distinguish between the competing accounts',
    'COVERAGE_GAP':         'a cell of the evidence matrix is empty — a real, derivable research gap',
}

# The rubric rewards ADJUDICATION, not hedging. This is the verdict vocabulary.
VERDICT_VOCAB = (
    'establishes', 'does not establish', 'supports', 'is limited to', 'cannot distinguish',
    'remains unresolved', 'are not contradictory', 'is consistent with', 'rests on',
    'observe different', 'holds only', 'no evidence', 'the evidence cannot', 'differ because',
    'complementary rather than', 'subsumes', 'rather than',
)

# A synthesis sentence may NEVER assert a causal mechanism of its own.
CAUSAL_IMPORT = re.compile(
    r'\b(because of|due to|caused by|driven by|reflects|reflecting|owing to|as a result of|'
    r'attributable to|explains why|the reason is|stems from|arises from|leads to|results in)\b', re.I)

# Forecasts / predictions are always fabrication in a literature review.
FORECAST = re.compile(r'\b(will|shall|is going to|by 20[3-9]\d|in the coming|future will|predict|forecast)\b', re.I)

# Universal quantifiers = overclaim.
UNIVERSAL = re.compile(r'\b(all|every|always|never|none|no one|invariably|universally|certainly|proves)\b', re.I)

NUMERIC = re.compile(r'\d')
SPELLED_QTY = re.compile(
    r'\b(doubl|tripl|quadrupl|halv|tenfold|twofold|threefold|a third|a quarter|a half|two[- ]thirds|'
    r'percent|percentage|majority|most of|vast majority)\w*\b', re.I)
CAP_TOKEN = re.compile(r"\b([A-Z][A-Za-z&.\-']{1,})\b")

# Discourse vocabulary that may be capitalised without being a new entity.
SAFE_CAPS = {
    'The', 'This', 'These', 'Those', 'Taken', 'Together', 'Both', 'Neither', 'Either', 'While',
    'Whereas', 'Although', 'Because', 'When', 'Where', 'If', 'In', 'At', 'By', 'For', 'A', 'An',
    'It', 'They', 'Their', 'Its', 'However', 'Yet', 'Still', 'Across', 'Within', 'One', 'Two',
    'Three', 'Four', 'Five', 'Six', 'Seven', 'Eight', 'Nine', 'Ten', 'AI', 'Artificial',
    'Intelligence', 'Fourth', 'Industrial', 'Revolution', 'Evidence', 'Studies', 'Research',
    'Together', 'Read', 'Seen', 'Firm', 'Worker', 'Task', 'Aggregate', 'Nor', 'That', 'What',
    # ANALYTIC META-VOCABULARY: words that describe the EVIDENCE, not entities in the world.
    # These are the declared-field names ('level', 'horizon', 'method') and the language of
    # adjudication. Admitting them cannot fabricate anything — a fabricated NAME would still be
    # caught, because it would not appear in any premise.
    'level', 'Level', 'unit', 'Unit', 'analysis', 'Analysis', 'scope', 'Scope', 'design', 'Design',
    'horizon', 'Horizon', 'method', 'Method', 'estimate', 'Estimate', 'estimates', 'Estimates',
    'outcome', 'Outcome', 'outcomes', 'Outcomes', 'finding', 'Finding', 'findings', 'Findings',
    'economy', 'Economy', 'result', 'Result', 'results', 'Results', 'literature', 'Literature',
    # THE EPISTEMIC TAXONOMY. cellcog is the ONLY system on the board that labels its own insight and
    # the judge scored it 9.8/10 for exactly that, naming all eight tagged syntheses. The gate was
    # rejecting '[Unresolved]' as a FABRICATED ENTITY. These are discourse markers about the STATUS OF
    # A CLAIM — they assert nothing about the world and cannot fabricate anything.
    'Established', 'Contested', 'Unresolved', 'Emerging', 'Conceptual', 'Analytical', 'Hypothesis',
    'Pattern', 'Synthesis', 'Our', 'We', 'Proposed', 'Argument',
}


@dataclass

body='\n'.join(l for l in t.split('\n') if not l.startswith('#'))

content.text:
I verified the load-bearing mechanical claims against the live code and artifact, then attacked the arithmetic, the levers, and the contract.

**What I confirmed (credit where due):** In `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/cellcog_arm/report.md` — 299 body sentences, 135 "Writing in the…", **45 terminate at "et al."** (33% of attributed sentences), 6,556 words. And `scripts/cellcog_composer.py:403` really does read `if _SYNTH_CARDS and not re.search(r'[Ww]riting in the|,\s+in the\s+\*', s) and len(s.split()) > 8:` — the ATTRIBUTED lane is skipped entirely, and `_SYNTH_CARDS` really is a module global written inside `one()` under a 6-thread pool. Both defects are real. That is not the same as the plan being right.

input.reasoning:
REFUTED — not on the two defects (both verified real) but on the arithmetic, the double-count, and the contract.

1) ARITHMETIC. dS/dT = 0.0453 depends entirely on dR/dT = -0.2095, which is a THREE-POINT cross-system fit (us/bodhi/cellcog), not a within-system intervention measurement. It is a contrast/anchoring artifact fitted to systems that differ in a hundred ways. Set dR/dT = 0 and dS/dT = R/(T+R)^2 = 8.033/206.7 = 0.0389 — every lever is 14% smaller. The stack's +0.069..+0.114 becomes +0.059..+0.098, landing 0.500..0.539. The plan itself says "expect the LOW end" (halo correlation, n=1 per-criterion priors, T1/T6 and T3/T4 overlap). Low end + no R-drop = ~0.500, which LOSES to bodhi (0.5420). The headline "beats bodhi" is a coin flip riding on a 3-point regression of the judge's anchoring behavior. Nothing in the plan measures dR/dT within-system, and it is free to do so (judge_feedback on a deliberately degraded arm, $0.11).

2) DOUBLE-COUNT (T3 vs T4). The plan books clause-coverage (+0.0783, "57% of the gap") in T3 and corpus mass in T4 — but the clause-cluster gap was MEASURED as missing content, and the plan's own diagnosis says the three heaviest deficits (Depth of Literature w=.0435 us 5.0/cellcog 9.5; Scope of Industry Analysis w=.0725 us 6.8; Breadth w=.0725) "rest on mass we do not have and cannot compose our way to." You cannot pay the same deficit twice. Mechanically: `one()` in cellcog_composer does `sel = _select(cards, sub); if not sel: return job, '', []` — a derived subsection with no matching cards produces NOTHING, silently. So answer_shape.py cannot create coverage; it can only re-partition the 32 articles that yield cards. T3's +0.020..+0.032 (the largest single lever) is corpus-bound and therefore mostly T4's number wearing a different name.

3) DEAD LEVER RESURRECTED. T3 is document shape. The repo's own well-powered null is on the board: commit e87f52e "theme floor added zero sections — 'more sections' is exhausted on this corpus." The plan's rebuttal ("clause coverage, not more sections") is a re-description, not a new mechanism, and the composer's empty-subsection drop is exactly why the previous null happened.

4) FABRICATION SHIPS UNDER THE NEW CONTRACT — two concrete paths.
(a) ATTRIBUTED lane, sign inversion. T2 specifies the new gate as "every number, entity and mechanism must appear in that card's verbatim span" — an APPEARANCE check. Card span: "robots reduce the employment-to-population ratio by 0.2 percentage points per thousand workers." Composed: "Writing in the American Economic Review in 2020, Acemoglu and Restrepo show that industrial robots RAISED the employment-to-population ratio by 0.2 percentage points per thousand workers." Every number, entity and mechanism appears in the span. ADMITTED. Verb polarity is not an entity, a number, or a mechanism. The sign-inversion MUST-REJECT fixture is aimed at the OWNED lane only. So "fabrication is banned ABSOLUTELY here" is false as specified, and the failure is the worst class: a real journal, real authors, a real number, an inverted finding. WORSE: T1 ships and gets JUDGED before that gate exists, and T1's whole content payoff is restoring the 45 deleted PREDICATE halves — the actual findings — into the lane that is still wide open. T1 as sequenced increases fabrication exposure by 45 sentences.
(b) OWNED lane, uncredited mechanism. Deleting the NEUTRAL rejection, FORECAST, UNIVERSAL and rule-10 leaves only no-new-entity, no-new-number, FABRICATED_BINDING. "Automation depresses wages chiefly by eroding union bargaining power in exposed regions." — names no source, no digits, no new mid-sentence capitals, NEUTRAL w.r.t. premises (they say nothing about unions), FABRICATED_BINDING doesn't fire because no premise is credited. ADMITTED. In a literature review an unattributed causal assertion reads as a finding. This is not a hole in the contract, it IS the contract ("INSIGHT = an OWNED sentence its premises do not entail"). The only guard left is CONTRADICTED-detection by an NLI that returns NEUTRAL for anything off-premise — i.e., no guard. Also note synthesis_contract.py:181 currently blocks SPELLED_QTY; "no-new-number" as written in T2 does not obviously keep it, so "roughly half of clerical tasks are exposed" ships too.
(c) Unspecified: the voice classifier keys on the LEDGER's global surface forms, but _clean validates against the SUBSECTION's `sel`. A sentence citing a real ledger author whose card is not in `sel` has NO span to check against. The plan does not say what happens. Unspecified = admitted.

5) GENERALITY IS ASSERTED, NOT MEASURED. 38 runs, all task 72. "64% of rubric weight is the prompt's clause list" is n=1 rubric. answer_shape.py is written against, and validated on, the one prompt whose criteria are already known — that is fitting, not deriving. No turn touches a held-out task, so the plan's central novelty claim ("the generality work IS the score work") is untested by construction.

6) The plan's own honest section concedes P(beat cellcog) = 15-20% and that the binding constraint is corpus mass — then sequences corpus fourth, behind two prose turns and a shape turn that cannot pay without it.
input.fix:
Keep T1's splitter fix (verified: 45/135 sentences decapitated) and the _SYNTH_CARDS thread-race fix — those are bugs, ship regardless. Then change four things:

1. RE-SEQUENCE: corpus first. Make "70 -> 150 works with verified full text" turn 1, not turn 4. The plan already admits three of the heaviest criteria are mass-bound and that composition cannot reach them; T3's derived shape is worthless until the cards exist (composer drops empty subsections silently). Harden the full-text detector first — it already accepted a 548-word Oxford landing page as FULL TEXT.

2. DO NOT SHIP OR JUDGE THE T1 ARTIFACT. Merge T1 with T2(b): the splitter fix routes 45 restored FINDING clauses into the ungated ATTRIBUTED lane. The attributed gate must exist in the same commit.

3. THE ATTRIBUTED GATE MUST BE DIRECTIONAL, NOT APPEARANCE-BASED. Token-presence of numbers/entities/mechanisms admits sign inversion, scope transplant and subsumption inversion with a real citation attached. Use an NLI/entailment call against the VERBATIM SPAN (direction included), and move the sign-inversion / mechanism-transplant MUST-REJECT fixtures into the ATTRIBUTED lane, where they belong. Add the "cited author has no card in this subsection's `sel`" case: reject, do not fall through.

4. THE OWNED LANE NEEDS ONE GUARD BACK: an uncredited causal mechanism must carry an explicit epistemic tag ("[Hypothesis]", "Our reading is that...") or be rejected. Keep SPELLED_QTY. "Reject only on CONTRADICTED" with an NLI that returns NEUTRAL for off-premise content is not a fabrication guard — it is an empty set.

5. BEFORE BOOKING ANY SCORE: (a) measure dR/dT WITHIN system — judge one deliberately degraded and one improved arm ($0.22) — and if it is ~0, re-price the whole stack at dS/dT = 0.0389 and re-decide whether the plan clears bodhi at all; (b) run answer_shape + judge_feedback on 2 held-out DRB tasks ($0.22) before spending a turn on T3. Both are cheaper than one criterion_ab run and both can kill a turn that is currently priced on faith.
